title: pedicularis racemosa subsp. alba
intro: Pedicularis racemosa subsp. alba, also known as White Lousewort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the balsamroot family. It is native to the western coast of North America, where it is commonly found in moist, shaded areas. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. White Lousewort can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits also include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing due to its antiseptic properties.
